title: सीता स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सीता स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
सिंहासनगतां नित्यं पद्मपत्रनिभेक्षणाम्।
पद्मिनीं पद्महस्तां च सीतां वन्दे रघुप्रियाम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥

जनकनन्दिनि सीते जगन्मातर्नमोऽस्तु ते।
पतिव्रताप्रधाने त्वं सर्वलोकमहेश्वरि॥१॥

भूमिजे भूमिकन्ये च भूमिसम्भवदेवते।
मिथिलेशसुते सीते राघवप्रियवल्लभे॥२॥

वैदेहि जानकी सीता मैथिली भूसुता शुभा।
रामपत्नी महाभागा लक्ष्मीः सर्वगुणान्विता॥३॥

रामस्य दक्षिणे पार्श्वे या तिष्ठति सनातनी।
सा सीता परमेशानी सर्वलोकनमस्कृता॥४॥

अशोकवनमध्यस्था रावणेन निरूधिता।
रामध्यानपरा नित्यं न विचालितमानसा॥५॥

शुद्धा सती महासाध्वी अग्निपरीक्षितव्रता।
धैर्यधर्मदयायुक्ता सीता सौभाग्यदायिनी॥६॥

वनवासव्रतधरा कन्दमूलफलाशिनी।
पतिसेवापरा नित्यं राघवानन्ददायिनी॥७॥

लवकुशजननी माता सर्वलोकप्रिया सती।
वाल्मीकिआश्रमस्थाना राघवेशानसुन्दरी॥८॥

धरणीं प्रविशन्ती या स्वमातुःक्रोडमागता।
शुद्धिं दर्शयती लोके सीता सत्यपतिव्रता॥९॥

नमस्ते जानकी देवि नमस्ते रामवल्लभे।
नमस्ते सर्वलोकस्य जननि त्वां नमाम्यहम्॥१०॥

सीतायाः स्तवराजोऽयं सर्वकामफलप्रदः।
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वसौभाग्यवर्धनम्॥

सौमंगल्यं महत्पुण्यं पतिभक्तिविवर्धनम्।
आयुरारोग्यसौख्यं च सन्तानं लभते ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री वाल्मीकिरामायणोक्तं श्री सीता स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sita Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Simhasana-gatam Nityam Padma-patra-nibhekshanam.
Padminim Padma-hastam cha Sitam Vande Raghu-priyam.

|| Stotram ||

Janaka-nandini Site Jagan-matar-namo'stu Te.
Pativrata-pradhane Tvam Sarva-loka-maheshvari.||1||

Bhumije Bhumi-kanye cha Bhumi-sambhava-devate.
Mithilesha-sute Site Raghava-priya-vallabhe.||2||

Vaidehi Janaki Sita Maithili Bhusuta Shubha.
Rama-patni Mahabhaga Lakshmih Sarva-guna-anvita.||3||

Ramasya Dakshine Parshve Ya Tishthati Sanatani.
Sa Sita Parameshani Sarva-loka-namaskrita.||4||

Ashoka-vana-madhyastha Ravanena Nirudhita.
Rama-dhyana-para Nityam Na Vichalita-manasa.||5||

Shuddha Sati Mahasadhvi Agni-parikshita-vrata.
Dhairya-dharma-daya-yukta Sita Saubhagya-dayini.||6||

Vana-vasa-vrata-dhara Kanda-mula-phala-ashini.
Pati-seva-para Nityam Raghava-ananda-dayini.||7||

Lava-Kusha-janani Mata Sarva-loka-priya Sati.
Valmiki-ashrama-sthana Raghavesha-na-sundari.||8||

Dharanim Pravishanti Ya Sva-matuh-krodam-agata.
Shuddhim Darshayati Loke Sita Satya-pativrata.||9||

Namaste Janaki Devi Namaste Rama-vallabhe.
Namaste Sarva-lokasya Janani Tvam Namamy-aham.||10||

Sitayah Stava-rajo'yam Sarva-kama-phala-pradah.
Yah Pathet-pratar-utthaya Sarva-saubhagya-vardhanam.

Saumangalyam Mahat-punyam Pati-bhakti-vivardhanam.
Ayur-arogya-saukhyam cha Santanam Labhate Dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Valmiki-Ramayanaoktam Shri Sita Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे जनकनन्दिनी सीते! हे जगन्माता! आपको नमस्कार। पतिव्रताओं में प्रधान, सर्वलोकमहेश्वरी आप हैं।

हे भूमिजा! भूमिकन्या! भूमि से प्रकट देवता! मिथिलेश की पुत्री, राघव की प्रियवल्लभा सीते!

वैदेही, जानकी, सीता, मैथिली, भूसुता — ये सब आपके पवित्र नाम हैं। आप राम की पत्नी, महाभाग्यवती, लक्ष्मी स्वरूपा, सर्वगुणों से सम्पन्न हैं।

जो राम के दक्षिण पार्श्व में सनातनी रूप से विराजती हैं, वे सीता परमेशानी सर्वलोकनमस्कृत हैं।

अशोकवन में रावण द्वारा बन्दी रहते हुए भी जिनका मन राम-ध्यान से कभी विचलित नहीं हुआ। शुद्ध, सती, महासाध्वी, अग्निपरीक्षा उत्तीर्ण, धैर्य-धर्म-दया से युक्त, सौभाग्यदायिनी सीता।

लव-कुश की जननी, सर्वलोकप्रिया, वाल्मीकि आश्रम में निवास करने वाली। अपनी माता पृथ्वी की गोद में जाकर शुद्धि दर्शाने वाली सत्य-पतिव्रता सीता।

इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सौभाग्य, सौमंगल्य, पतिभक्ति, आयु, आरोग्य, सुख और सन्तान प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Supreme prayer to Sita Devi, the ideal of womanhood and devotion
Invokes marital bliss, saubhagya, and harmony in relationships
Invokes the blessing of progeny, health, longevity, and family happiness
Increases devotion to one's spouse and strengthens pativrata dharma
Invokes the combined grace of Lakshmi and Sita as consort of Rama
Especially beneficial for married women seeking domestic well-being
description: Sita Stotram — सीता स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Sita. Rooted in Valmiki Ramayana tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to increase devotion to one's spouse and strengthen pativrata dharma and supreme prayer to Sita Devi, the ideal of womanhood and devotion. It is also said to grant marital bliss, saubhagya, and harmony in relationships. For best results, devotees chant this on Friday (Shukravar) and during Ram Navami and Vivah Panchami. At 10 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Sita Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sita Stotram and where does it come from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sita Stotram (सीता स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise to Goddess Sita Devi — the consort of Lord Rama and the embodiment of pativrata dharma — preserved in the Valmiki Ramayana tradition and widely recited in classical Vaishnava worship. The ten-verse stotra invokes Sita through her many sacred names — Janaki (daughter of Janaka), Vaidehi (princess of Videha), Maithili (princess of Mithila), Bhumija (born of the earth), Bhusuta (daughter of Bhumi Devi) — and venerates her as Jagat-mata (mother of the universe), Lakshmi-svarupa, and the supreme exemplar of devotion to one's spouse. It celebrates her vana-vasa, her endurance in Ashoka-vana under Ravana's captivity, the agni-pariksha, and her final return to her mother Earth. (Source: Valmiki Ramayana stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Sita and what do her many names mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Sita Devi is the eternal consort of Lord Rama, an avatara of Goddess Lakshmi, and the divine ideal of feminine virtue, devotion and sacrifice in Hindu tradition. Her many names each carry sacred meaning: Sita means furrow (she emerged from a furrow when King Janaka was ploughing the sacred field), Janaki means daughter of Janaka, Vaidehi means princess of the Videha kingdom, Maithili means princess of Mithila, Bhumija and Bhusuta mean born of the Earth (her mother is Bhumi Devi), and Rama-vallabha means the beloved of Rama. Each name is a meditation on a different aspect of her divinity — earthly origin, royal lineage, devotion to Rama, and her ultimate identity as Lakshmi. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana; Sita Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Sita Stotram be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sita Stotram is most powerful when chanted on Friday (Shukravar — sacred to Lakshmi-Sita), and especially during Vivah Panchami (Margashirsha Shukla Panchami — the celestial wedding day of Sita and Rama at Janakpur), Sita Navami (Vaishakha Shukla Navami — Sita's appearance day from the earth at Mithila), Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami), and during Navaratri. Married women traditionally recite it daily before the morning lamp for marital harmony, saubhagya (auspicious wifehood) and the long life of the husband. The phalashruti promises sarva-saubhagya, mahat-punya, devotion, longevity, health, comfort and progeny. (Sources: Sita Stotram phalashruti; Drikpanchang Sita Navami Vivah Panchami calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Sita Stotram bestow on the devotee?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to regular recitation of the Sita Stotram: (1) saumangalya — auspiciousness in family life, (2) mahat-punya — accumulation of great spiritual merit, (3) pati-bhakti-vivardhanam — strengthening of devotion between husband and wife, (4) ayur-arogya-saukhyam — long life, health and physical comfort, (5) santanam labhate dhruvam — assured progeny (especially valuable for couples seeking children), (6) sarva-kama-phala-pradah — fulfilment of all righteous desires, and (7) the supreme gift of grace from Lakshmi-Sita herself. It is considered especially efficacious for married women, women seeking marriage, and couples desiring family happiness. (Source: Sita Stotram phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Sita Stotram different from the Sita Chalisa or Sita Ashtottara?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are devotional offerings to Sita Devi but differ in length, language and ritual purpose. The Sita Stotram is a Sanskrit ten-verse classical stotra preserved in the Valmiki Ramayana tradition, suitable for short daily recitation and traditional puja. The Sita Chalisa is a 40-verse Awadhi/Hindi devotional composition in the popular bhakti tradition (modelled on the Hanuman Chalisa pattern), accessible to non-Sanskrit devotees and especially loved in north India. The Sita Ashtottara Shatanamavali is a 108-name litany used for archana — offering one flower or kumkum dab per name — during formal worship. The Stotram is for stuti, the Chalisa for bhakti-katha, and the Ashtottara for archana. (Source: classical Sita devotional literature.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the verse describing Sita's captivity in Ashoka-vana?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 of the Sita Stotram — Ashoka-vana-madhyastha Ravanena Nirudhita, Rama-dhyana-para Nityam Na Vichalita-manasa — celebrates one of the most sacred episodes in the Ramayana. While imprisoned by Ravana in the Ashoka grove of Lanka and surrounded by demonesses, Sita's mind never wavered for a single moment from meditation on Rama. This verse is recited as a meditation on unwavering devotion (achala-bhakti) — the supreme example of how the heart, when fully fixed on the Beloved, remains untouched even by the most violent external circumstances. It is held up in tradition as the perfect ideal of pativrata-dharma and ekanta-bhakti. (Source: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda; Sita Stotram verse 5.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Sita identified with Lakshmi in the Sita Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 3 of the Sita Stotram explicitly declares Sita as Lakshmih Sarva-guna-anvita — Lakshmi herself, endowed with all virtues. In Vaishnava and especially Sri Vaishnava tradition, Sita is understood as the very avatara of Goddess Lakshmi who descended with Lord Vishnu (as Rama) to participate in the lila of dharma-rakshana. The Ramayana itself indicates this in multiple places, and the Sita Upanishad and Lakshmi Sahasranama both identify Sita with Lakshmi. By praising Sita, the devotee simultaneously invokes the grace of Lakshmi for prosperity (Lakshmi-aspect), the grace of Sita for marital and familial harmony (consort-aspect), and the grace of Bhumi Devi for stability and patience (earth-mother aspect). (Sources: Sita Upanishad; Sri Vaishnava theology; Valmiki Ramayana.)
